reviews.review_msg: I took BUS315&413 with Emrul in 2021 Spring and Summer. He is the best finance teacher I have met in my life. He not only taught me a wide range of finance materials, covering the majority of the CFA curriculum, which built me a solid financial foundation for my future life but also strongly cared and helped me in life whenever I needed help.

Emrul is an amazing prof, probably one of the best I have had at SFU. Regarding the structure of the class, there is an individual assignment, group project, midterm, final, and participation/attendance marks. Tests can be tough, but are very easy to prepare for - he gives a good heads up on what to focus on.

Emrul's classes are't easy because the content itself is tough grasp. If you pay attention in class, take notes and do practice questions, you will do well. He will always take time to explain concepts if you are having trouble and cares about his students. Formula sheets aren't given in exams, but you will survive without it. Great prof overall!

Emrul is the reason why I chose Finance as a career. His lectures are extremely informative and also enjoyable. He truly cares about his students as he always gives us advice on how to prep for interviews and life in general. Not easy by any means, need to show up to do well, but you will get the most out of his courses vs any other. UBC is lucky

BEST PROF EVER. Hilarious lectures and very knowledgeable. Explained concepts in an easily understandable way. Exams are a fair game, so class participation really matters! Emrul truly cares about his students. Goes beyond during office hours by giving career tips and advice if it's your last semester. Recommend 100%

Emrul is my favourite prof at SFU. I took 2 courses with him: one econ and one finance and he teaches both with proper diligence. His courses are tough, but he makes them easy my explaining well. He is also always available when needed.
Emrul is the best deal! He is never boring in the lectures. The content was difficult for me but I liked the way he explained things. Highly recoomended if you want to do CFA in future.
Emrul is the best prof I had at SFU. He motivates students to a level that you start loving the subject materials. The course was very tough, but he made it enjoyable.
WORST PROF EVER. AVOID.

one of the most inspirational lecturer at SFU! take BUS 413 with him! you will learn a lot although you are going to die in the process due to so much material that he covers. but it is all very practical and important! would definitely take his class again! he has such great tenacity in the success of his students, which is AWSOME!

My second time taking class with Emrul. He is nice and gives amazing lectures. He will tell you the difference of using the concepts in studying and in real life, and also gives real examples. For this class, u need to put a lot effort to have a good grade, but what u learned is very useful.

Emrul is a very engaging and enthusiastic professor.  He cares greatly about the learning of his students, in terms of how the course material will help them in their future careers.  This is why all of the lecture material and assignments are extremely  practical and similar to the work one would be doing as a financial analyst.

Emrul is the best prof I have ever seen in my SFU career. He makes finance really fun even though the course is really tough. I didn't really like finance until I took his class. He inspired me and showed me the real world of finance. Highly recommended.

Emrul is not bad overall despite a few things. His course is a little bit disorganized and he sometimes makes mistakes. The instructions for the assignment and project are not clear and he doesn't want questions for the project. However, the good thing is that the MT and final are fair as long as you attend lectures, pay attention. Would recommend

Very good prof for the class, makes the material easy to understand and retain the information. His midterm and final are not too difficult given that you study his slides. Make sure you take notes and attend every lecture, since he might give pop quizzes. Most useful finance class I've taken and probably one of the best profs for corporate finance

One of the best instructor I've ever encountered in Beedie. Very support, always be there for his students. He teaches a lot of practical stuffs, which gets students ready for the real world (As a final year student you would appreciate this a lot). Grading is very fair, you get exactly what you deserved. last but not least, great sense of humor!!

He is funny and teaches with a lot of real life example. Gives career advice during the last class that's very helpful. Always there to help. I'm graduating, otherwise I would like to take more classes with him.
One of the best profs I've had at SFU. Good sense of humor. Very organized. His lectures are fun and very informative.  Choose his class and "you will thank me later".
Enjoyed his lecture very much. He gave many real life examples and taught practical stuffs. Good sense of humor. Career advice at the last class was very helpful.
Great Professor, the lecture is fun and educational. Very helpful!!! Real life projects and experiences
good prof, makes hard concept a lot easier than reading the textbook. fair exam gives you a lot of heads up about what's coming.  I would take his class again :D

Great Professor, is obviously passionate about finance. I enjoyed the lectures, he is knowledgeable and brought real world experience to what he was talking about. He covers a lot of material but outlines what is expected very clearly.
Hard to understand. Poor teaching skills.

One of the best business profs I had at SFU, materials he taught was very practical. Friendly guy if you “participate” in his in class discussions. He will remember who does. Extremely selective about presentations, be prepared to answer questions about tiny details. But if he remembers you from the class discussions, it's a much easier course.
book is only useful for assignments. notes are very clear. give enough practice questions and clear answer. always available for help. the whole course is easy except the final.

He is the best professor in my entire life. He explained concepts clearly with excellent examples. He repeated what he said sometimes, but it helped you to remember it, and you will also know it is an important topic. He is way too nice to his students. Even if he is very smart, he always teaches and thinks from students' side. He is the prof that

Very helpful in answering your questions (only face-to-face since he doesn't reply emails). Explained concepts well (I liked how he repeated what he said a few times). Exams were fair - the structure was very similar to sample exams so you have to do them in order to do well.

He is clear in everything he says, because he repeats the same sentence in three different ways so it sticks to your mind!  Complicated concepts explained clearly.  Helpful when approached with meaningful questions.  Overall, best finance prof one can get.
Excellent teacher
He's my favorite teacher
Great prof!  Very good at actually TEACHING, rather than lecturing everyone to death.  Best finance prof at SFU.
He's a great teacher and one of the best profs I've had at SFU.  Knows his stuff.
He's good at teaching...but the stuff he teaches is quite hard...overall, he is the best compared to other finance profs.
No Comments

A very bad prof. Very disorganized. Notes are very bad, and very bad hand writing. With these suck ups in class, always go off topic. Spend too much time in the beginning of semester to teach useless stuff.

Terrible finance professor.  Highly disorganized and archaic in his teaching methods.  Unavailable for help and never responds by email or phone, since that technology is obviously too advanced for his mind.

Awesome guy. May not be the most organized, but he has great qualities. He loves Finance and talks with passion. If you need help, he is always there. He is really smart and exams are very fair! They are not easy and not too hard - they are just fair. If you do all the work he tells you to, there will be no surprises. He is good!

Pros: detailed notes, keeps stressing his points
 Con: Wastes no time in making u feel stupid.  Im a hard worker & asked him some Q's during his office hrs, and he laughed, said its "elementary stuff, how can u not get this?"  I felt like punching him. Theres more but only 300 character limit.
Although his exams are difficult, he teaches really well and he is really nice professor

Really good professor. Very very helpful. He knows the course material very well.  Excellent prof. to learn finance from! Exams are not too difficult although they require some thought and clear understanding of course material. must attend lecture
Great lectures. VERY clear,difficult exams though.If you want to learn something take it with this professor/

Definitely quiet, but as long as everyone else is quiet, it shouldn't be a problem. Definitely very knowledgeable, and is willing to help you. Tests are mostly based on the practice questions. Show up to the lectures; he literally tells you what he'll be testing you on, so make sure you take note of which slides to study.

He is really boring; only big finance enthusiasts can listen to him for the entire lecture. He is not even trying to engage everyone or let us work in pairs/groups or participate in any way. He is very academic - all his example come from the papers he read; not real work. Throws tons of information at you but all you need is numeric tasks.
Smart guy, but when he talks, it's difficult to hear him well. Make sure you sit in the front row otherwise you may easily get distracted especially when he doesn't speak very clearly

He's knowledgeable, but could try to spice things up a little. He gives 4 "pop" quizes, but you can tell or he'll tell you when but they're quite difficult. You must pay attention to the details in his class and you'll do well. Overall, he's a solid teacher, I'd recommend him. (I did poorly on all his quizes but pulled off B overall with the final)
No Comments
No Comments

the course itself is interesting but even though the prof is knowledgeable, his presentation method is so phlegmatic that it puts me to sleep... but you have to work hard and stay on top on things. practice questions  done in class give a good indication of what to expect on the modterm.
overall, not bad.

Patrick is one of the best professors at SFU for Finance. He has extensive industry experience through running his own boutique investment firm. He focuses helping students understand core and nececessary concepts and not just regurgitating the text. Strongly recommended for any student who wants to succeed in Finance, to learn from Patrick.

Patrick is very knowledgeable and easy-going. Gives sufficient feedback on exercises. He talks about many real life examples (e.g. projects he has done) to show how the textbook knowledge can be applied in real world. He cares about the analysis of financial data, which we need to know when go to work. definitely enjoyed his class.
i found him a very good professor. He was always available to explain when I did not grasp the concepts myself.
Use the textbook to study for tests; there are questions that he does not prepare you for. Quizzes are relatively easy.

You get out what you put in. While I had to spend A LOT of time and work on this class, I learned SO MUCH and got a good grade. He expects students to do more than just show up to class to understand the concepts as they are involved. Study by yourself, keep up with the project, ask questions. He isn't that bad if you put the time in.

Midterm was similar to class examples but final was much harder because all topics discussed since Wk 1 were tested. The group project was a LOT of work and don't think you can finish it in 2 wks & get an A. I got average on midterm but pulled up my grade cause I studied hard for the final & constantly asked for his feedback while writing the paper

Best Professor Ever. I always sat in the first row in his lectures to hear him better. 100 % clear explanations to every single piece. Very good teacher. I was encouraged a lot. 100% accessible. I used to have many many questions. But he was always nice and patient to answer questions. Very very nice prof.
